title: erodium neuradifolium var. linosae
intro: Erodium neuradifolium var. linosae, also known as Greek or wild storksbill, is a plant species within the geranium family. The plant's roots and leaves are used for their medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Erodium neuradifolium var. linosae include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Erodium neuradifolium var. linosae is typically prepared as a tea or infusate, with some herbal remedies also incorporating it into tinctures or ointments.
